title: Lecture Two Hall Current From Chern-Simons Context
summary: This equation context records the functional-derivative route from the integer-level Chern-Simons effective action to the transverse Hall current J_2 = k E_1 / 2pi.
assumptions: The topological response dominates the low-energy effective action.
The sample occupies the x_1-x_2 plane.
regime: Macroscopic integer quantum Hall response theory.
scope: Equation-level context for the Hall current extracted from the Chern-Simons action.

source_anchors.notes: The functional derivative of the Chern-Simons response produces the Hall current.

representation: Equation context for the response-theory step that converts the topological action into the observed Hall conductivity.

math_expressions.notes: Definition of the induced current in the effective-action formalism.
The integer Hall system is governed by an effective Chern-Simons term.
The longitudinal electric field becomes a transverse Hall current.

symbols.meaning: Hall current flowing in the x_2 direction
electric field in the x_1 direction

retrieval_hints: Use when an answer must show the variation step and not only quote the final conductivity.
